lede: A look at November 4 through the almanac — historic events, famous birthdays including Darja Varfolomeev and Adrian Zaugg, and the day's notable deaths and observances.

events.title: UPS Airlines Flight 2976
events.desc: UPS Airlines Flight 2976, a McDonnell Douglas MD-11F crashes into multiple buildings during takeoff at Louisville Muhammad Ali International Airport in Louisville, Kentucky, killing 15 people, including the 3 crew members.

events.title: Barack Obama
events.desc: Barack Obama becomes the first person of biracial or African-American descent to be elected as President of the United States.

events.title: He Depu
events.desc: Chinese authorities arrest cyber-dissident He Depu for signing a pro-democracy letter to the 16th Communist Party Congress.

events.title: Iberia Flight 062
events.desc: Iberia Flight 062 crashes in Blackdown, West Sussex, killing all 37 people on board including British actress June Thorburn.

events.title: Adolf Hitler
events.desc: World War II: Disobeying a direct order by Adolf Hitler, General Field Marshal Erwin Rommel begins a retreat of his forces after a costly defeat during the Second Battle of El Alamein. The retreat would ultimately last five months.

events.title: World War II
events.desc: World War II: U.S. president Franklin D. Roosevelt orders the United States Customs Service to implement the Neutrality Act of 1939, allowing cash-and-carry purchases of weapons by belligerents.

events.title: City and South London Railway
events.desc: City and South London Railway: London's first deep-level tube railway opens between King William Street and Stockwell.

events.title: Camagüey
events.desc: Camagüey, Cuba, revolts against Spain during the Ten Years' War.

events.title: American Civil War
events.desc: American Civil War: Confederate troops bombard a Union supply base and destroy millions of dollars in materiel at the Battle of Johnsonville.

events.title: Teatro di San Carlo
events.desc: The Teatro di San Carlo, the oldest working opera house in Europe, is inaugurated in Naples, Italy.

births.name: Traian Băsescu
births.line: Romanian captain and politician, 4th President of Romania

births.name: Michael Wilson (Canadian politician)
births.line: Canadian academic and politician, 31st Canadian Minister of Finance (died 2019)

births.name: Eric Thompson (racing driver)
births.line: English race car driver and businessman (died 2015)

deaths.text: Bernard Marcus, American billionaire businessman and philanthropist
Yitzhak Rabin, Israeli general and politician, 5th Prime Minister of Israel, Nobel Peace Prize laureate
Robert E. Sherwood, American playwright and screenwriter

observances.text: Christian feast day: Charles Borromeo (Roman Catholic Church)
Christian feast day: Our Lady of Kazan (Russian Orthodox Church)
